items.title: State of Origin: Why did Billy Slater leave Reece Walsh out of his Game I Queensland Maroons team?
items.summary: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.

items.title: Vitrinite employees owed $16 million as Vulcan mine bids come due

items.title: Nicola Spurrier shares road safety message after she was hit by car while cycling

items.title: Tasmanian polling shows One Nation would win a 'number of seats' in next state election

items.title: Man pleads not guilty to murder but guilty to manslaughter in park amputation trial

Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.

items.title: Mature Artists Dance Experience for over 50s hopes for increased arts funding ahead of Tasmanian budget
items.summary: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.

items.title: Five men share the stories behind their beloved hats
items.summary: For many, hats are more than a way to keep the sun off your face; they tell stories and hold memories from far and wide. Five men share their collections.

items.title: Contempt proceedings launched against MP Mark Latham following social media outburst
items.summary: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.

items.title: US President Donald Trump, Chinese President Xi Jinping end unipolar age in Beijing
items.summary: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

items.title: A tiny nesting box in Ireland is my antidote to the despair of adulthood
items.summary: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

items.title: Cruel dismissal denies Mitch Marsh IPL century as Lucknow Super Giants defeat Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Mitch Marsh receives a nightmare slice of luck after a stunning innings put him on course for the triple figures.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein's third New York rape trial ends in mistrial
items.summary: Harvey Weinstein's third trial in New York on a charge that he raped aspiring actress Jessica Mann has ended in a mistrial, after a jury failed to reach a unanimous verdict.

items.title: Jonas Vingegaard conquors Blockhaus to take control of 2026 Giro d'Italia, Jai Hindley 3rd on stage
items.summary: Jonas Vingegaard stamped his authority on the Giro d'Italia, but three Aussies are in the top 10 overall and all remain in consideration for the podium.

items.title: SANFL kicking goals with growing viewership as AFL draws criticism
items.summary: SANFL crowds are continuing to climb each year after COVID-19 restrictions put a dent in numbers, but its broadcast reach is winning ratings, and streaming has reached new heights.

items.title: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war
items.summary: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

items.title: Counting underway in by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford, swing towards LNP
items.summary: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focused on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

items.title: How Wallabies winger Dylan Pietsch uses art to connect with his culture
items.summary: While Dylan Pietsch is a star on the rugby field for the Wallabies and Western Force, he turns to his love of painting to express himself and be creative.

items.title: Diphtheria used to be a major cause of childhood deaths. Now it's back in Australia
items.summary: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

items.title: Migrant nurses training to fill skills gaps in rural SA health project
items.summary: The rural town of Cleve in SA is getting a much-needed boost to its health system with a pilot program sponsoring five nurses from the Philippines to undertake traineeships to become enrolled nurses.

items.title: Road safety expert says country 'decades behind' after second fatal Gumlu bus crash
items.summary: A retired traffic engineer says Australia is "decades behind" in road safety standards and needs an overhaul after a second fatal bus crash on the same stretch of the Bruce Highway.

items.title: Young Australian Rules players eye US college football instead of AFL draft
items.summary: A new generation of Australian athletes is skipping the MCG entirely, eyeing US college football scholarships instead of Aussie Rules.

items.title: How WA's once-revered donkeys became 'nuisance' pests and canned meat
items.summary: Before roads and vehicles, teams of donkeys used to haul huge loads across remote parts of Western Australia. But the beloved animal became a declared pest that even the meat trade couldn't save.

items.title: Federal budget allocates $40m to fund an overhaul of airline passenger rights
items.summary: The federal government has announced nearly $40 million to fund an overhaul of aviation consumer rights.

items.title: Natural burials begin at NSW conservation cemetery Walawaani Way
items.summary: After nearly 10 years getting public health and council approvals, a natural burial ground on the NSW south coast begins letting people have a simpler, more environmentally friendly farewell.

items.title: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy
items.summary: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

items.title: Bronze bust of Scott Morrison joins Prime Ministers Avenue in Ballarat
items.summary: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.

items.title: The Interview: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools
items.summary: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

items.title: Wayne Swan slams the 'view from Mar-a-Lago' as battle for middle Australia gets real
items.summary: Labor and the Coalition are both acutely mindful of One Nation's rise and its determination to take a decisive role in a future conservative government.

items.title: Sam Coulton turned his family's cotton crop into a clothing brand now stocked across Australia
items.summary: Queensland cotton farmer and entrepreneur Sam Coulton has spent decades diversifying beyond agriculture, building a fashion brand and tourism business in Goondiwindi while creating jobs and investing in the future of his rural town.

items.title: Most widespread May rain in a decade to reach all Australian states
items.summary: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.

items.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group
items.summary: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

items.title: New Hollywood film excites as Philippine culture hits the big screen
items.summary: As animated film Forgotten Island gets ready to hit the big screen later this year, could it signal an end to Filipino typecasting in Hollywood?

items.title: What to watch in May, from free speech fodder to Nic Cage as Spider-Noir
items.summary: Plus, Shaun Micallef dives into Australia's gambling problem and the Stranger Things creators back another sci-fi mystery.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a stone cold 90s club banger
items.summary: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Auckland FC cruises past Adelaide United to reach maiden A-League Men's grand final
items.summary: Auckland becomes the first team from New Zealand to qualify for an A-League Men's season decider after making light work of Adelaide at Hindmarsh Stadium.

items.title: Five Italians die following cave diving incident in the Maldives
items.summary: The group was exploring a series of underwater caves at a depth of about 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll in the Maldives.

items.title: Swans hold on for gritty victory over Pies, Suns see off Port
items.summary: The ladder-leading Sydney Swans scrape past an undermanned but plucky Collingwood side, as Gold Coast defeats Port Adelaide in Darwin.

items.title: Developer David Trask bids $50,000 donation for a lunch with Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie
items.summary: The developer, David Trask, made the successful bid as a donation at lunch on Friday for the ruling LNP, held at the Brisbane Convention Centre and dubbed "Delivering for Queensland Industry Update".

items.title: Invasive 'barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected away from entry ports on mainland for first time
items.summary: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.

items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Costs under 'pressure' for controversial Perth Park racetrack project, state treasurer says
items.summary: Despite assurances to the contrary, WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti has conceded it would be difficult for the government to stick to the $217.5 million budget for the Perth Park project.

items.title: Chloroform suspected as reason Launceston General Hospital ward shut, staff ill, documents reveal
items.summary: An incident that prompted a Tasmanian hospital theatre to evacuate, and left several staff feeling ill, was possibly caused by chloroform, documents reveal.

items.title: Bennett insists Latrell will be fit for Origin despite injury exit in loss to Dolphins
items.summary: South Sydney coach Wayne Bennett says he expects Latrell Mitchell to take his place in State of Origin I despite the star centre hobbling off late in the Rabbitohs' Magic Round clash with the Dolphins.

items.title: Massage therapist sentenced to more than 13 years' jail for sexual abuse of 61 victims
items.summary: Two women who were sexually assaulted by an unqualified massage therapist have implored women to not feel afraid to "speak up" if they're ever in the same situation, after he was sentenced to more than 13 years in prison.

items.title: NSW coroner acknowledges "powerful presence" of Clare Nowland's family at inquest

items.summary: The man allegedly responsible for a shooting in Tasmania's north west on Wednesday has been charged with attempted murder.

items.summary: Western Australia's top judges have released a public notice saying many courthouses in regional and remote areas will close for at least three months due to a lack of security.

items.title: Universities warn against Coalition plan to cut foreign student numbers in migration crackdown
items.summary: Universities are bracing for foreign student numbers to absorb the bulk of cuts under a Coalition migration plan, with experts warning skilled visas would be difficult to cull.

items.title: Speeding driver who struck and killed novelist Brenda Walker jailed for four years

items.title: Socceroos star Mo Toure says he went with a hunch by moving to Norwich City

items.title: Former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews to be immortalised with statue
items.summary: The government says work is already underway to honour the state's longest-serving Labor premier.

items.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
items.summary: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.

items.title: How to kill off your lawn and plant a native garden instead
items.summary: Many of us are looking to create a native garden as a lower maintenance, more sustainable and often cheaper alternative.

items.title: ABC boss Hugh Marks flags TV program cuts as broadcaster pivots to new platforms
items.summary: The ABC's managing director says the public broadcaster's biggest challenge is "stopping things" as he seeks to grow audiences on new platforms without increasing staff.

items.title: Four students in hospital after suspected drug 'overdose' at Central Coast high school
items.summary: Fifteen students at a Central Coast high school have been assessed by paramedics, with four being taken to hospital.

items.title: Court upholds discrimination ruling on appeal after transgender woman excluded from Giggle for Girls app
items.summary: ​A landmark Federal Court ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app constituted unlawful gender identity discrimination is upheld on appeal.

items.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers get another two weeks pay reprieve
items.summary: Workers at Australia's only manganese smelter will be thrown an additional lifeline as the state and federal governments step in with a two-week pay offer. It comes days before a previous short-term deal was due to expire.

items.title: Sydney and Fremantle lead early flag chase, but dual styles make Hawthorn a strong chance
items.summary: An early review of the AFL season so far points to a split between two distinct styles, with one side making a case for being flag favourites by showing it can deliver both.

items.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say
items.summary: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

items.title: Flight with Australian and New Zealand passengers from hantavirus cruise ship arrives in Perth
items.summary: A flight carrying six people who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship lands at an air force base in Perth.

items.title: Sydney doctor Andrew Small charged with alleged sexual assault of patients
items.summary: Sydney GP Andrew Small is granted bail, after being charged with allegedly sexually assaulting multiple female patients at an inner-city medical centre.

items.title: Carlton interim coach Josh Fraser not ready to take full-time role after Michael Voss exit
items.summary: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

items.title: Federal Court rejects northern cattle industry's appeal over impact of live export ban
items.summary: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

items.title: Culture, gender and birth order meant I would always be in the 'sandwich generation'
items.summary: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

items.title: Calls for drought declaration as southern Queensland graziers begin to destock
items.summary: Graziers start destocking to preserve dwindling feed with rainfall well below average across the region.

items.title: Angus Taylor's budget reply shows times have changed since Peter Dutton
items.summary: To appreciate how much times have changed, it is worth comparing the opposition leader's budget reply speech to Peter Dutton's.

items.title: Spirit of Tasmania operator TT-Line to get $506 million government bailout amid bungled ferry rollout
items.summary: The announcement comes as TT-Line continues to wrestle with the financial fallout from its bungled delivery of the new Spirit of Tasmania vessels.

items.title: Matthew Wale voted in as new Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot
items.summary: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

items.title: National Commissioner for Indigenous children says people chosen to lead NT child protection review won't 'cut it'
items.summary: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

items.title: Australia vs Ireland AFLW clash locked in for North Sydney Oval in August
items.summary: Australia and Ireland will meet in an international women's match under Australian rules to kick off the 2026 AFLW season.

items.title: Manjimup's migrant community unite to mourn deaths of Vanuatu farm workers in horror crash
items.summary: Residents of a WA country town have come together to mourn the deaths of two Pacific island farm workers as investigations into their deaths continue.

items.title: Driver dies after car rolls over onto railway line at Kulangoor
items.summary: A 28-year-old man has died after a serious crash on the Sunshine Coast this morning.

items.title: Victorian country firefighters slam bureaucracy for slashing roadside burns by 90pc
items.summary: Victorian fire authorities release new findings that show how planned burns helped control and contain the devastating summer bushfires.
